title: selaginella alutacea
intro: Selaginella alutacea, also known as the black lily or black spore moss, is a type of small, delicate, and rare fern-like plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Selaginella alutacea are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the body. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, taken in capsule form, or applied topically as a poultice or salve to alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
